description: Honesty eval fixtures for the `route` recipe. Each case is a labeled request with the destination route SHOULD reach. `trap` names the failure direction the case guards against (or null for a clean case). These are a calibration dataset for evaluating the route recipe's decisions; the route decision itself is made by an agent following help/route.txt, so these are not asserted against a code classifier — the coverage test only checks the dataset stays balanced across routes and both failure directions.

cases.reason: ESLint's packaged no-console already covers this; claiming it for a Taskless rule erodes trust where Taskless would never win.
Formatter / eslint-plugin-import territory, not a semantic rule.
@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any is a packaged rule; enable it rather than authoring.

cases.request: write a Ruff rule for X (repo has ruff.toml)
cases.reason: Naming a tool must ENGAGE routing into that tool, not suppress the skill.

cases.request: add an ESLint rule banning direct process.env reads outside config/
cases.reason: User named ESLint and wants a custom rule; author it in ESLint's dialect, mined from the repo's own rules.

cases.request: controllers must not import the db module directly
cases.reason: Bespoke syntactic import ban, no packaged rule, cleanly expressible as a local ast-grep rule.

cases.request: never call fetch directly — use our http wrapper
cases.reason: Call/import ban; classic local ast-grep pattern.

cases.request: enforce our feature-flag naming convention ff_*
cases.reason: Repo-specific identifier pattern is a simple local ast-grep rule; escalating to the service here wastes a generation and a login.

cases.request: flag string literals that look like API keys in source files
cases.reason: A regex-shaped syntactic match is locally solvable; do not route to remote just because it sounds security-ish.

cases.request: every API handler must parse its request body through a Zod schema before using it
cases.reason: Cross-statement intent (parse-before-use) that a single ast-grep pattern strains to express; let the service classify static vs runtime.

cases.request: flag any PR that adds a new env var without documenting it in the README
cases.reason: Cross-file, config-vs-docs consistency — needs a runtime check.ts the service generates.

cases.request: ensure every exported function in a route file has a matching test in __tests__
cases.reason: Cross-file correlation no static syntactic matcher expresses; service/runtime territory.
